interview.skip_reason: Scope doc already resolved problem statement and motivation

technical_leakage.details: No schemas, APIs, architecture, or implementation details surfaced in interview questions. Codebase was explored silently; findings presented as product behavior (e.g., 'The app currently has three roles' not 'The permissions.ts file defines ac.newRole'). Customer model fields referenced by product name (first name, email) not by column name.

issues: Public waiver route must bypass authenticated org layout -- acknowledged constraint, not a PRD defect
NFR-6 (no-JS degradation) may be unrealistic for rich text display -- suggest softening

trigger_conditions_met: PRD introduces 10+ domain nouns not in codebase (waiver template, template version, signed waiver, waiver status, expiration period, e-signature, audit metadata, guardian, minor, waiver coverage, signing page)
Naming conflict: 'waiver' refers to both template and signed record
Naming conflict: 'member' overloaded between staff role and customer membership
Feature crosses bounded context: authenticated staff area to public customer-facing area

next_step.reason: Feature has significant UI surface area: public signing page (first public-facing page), template editor with rich text, waiver status badges in existing customer table, guardian/minor flow. UI design decisions needed before architecture.

product_findings: Three roles: owner, admin, member with granular permissions
waiver_template and waiver already defined as permission resources
Customer model has all fields needed for waiver signer info
No public-facing pages exist yet -- all routes require auth
Settings uses tabbed layout pattern (locations, team, billing)
UI: shadcn/Radix components, Card-based layouts, Badge for status
Organization has name, logo, branding JSON in settings
